subject: Computer Networking
title: How URLs Work
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How URLs Work.

hook.text: Every page on the web has an address. URLs are the coordinates that take you to any resource online.

buildup.text: A URL has parts: scheme (https), host (example.com), path (/page), and optional query (?key=value).

discovery.text: The browser resolves the host to an IP via DNS, then uses the path to request a specific resource.

twist.text: Percent encoding handles special characters. A space becomes %20. That's why URLs look ugly sometimes.

climax.text: Fragment identifiers (#section) navigate within a page. They never leave the browser — the server never sees them.

punchline.text: URLs address every resource on the web. Simple, universal, powerful.

quiz.question: What part of a URL does the server never see?
options: The fragment identifier after the # symbol
